user_query:
I save local event announcements related to math lectures and alumni activities (I studied under Ge Molin) as HTML or Markdown in input/announcements/. Please set up an event-triggered incremental parser that, on each run, processes only new announcement files and writes structured outputs. Treat "new" as: any file in input/announcements/ that is not listed in input/processed_state.json under processed_paths. Requirements:

1) Parse all *.html and *.md files under input/announcements/ that are not already listed in input/processed_state.json processed_paths. Extract at minimum: title, date (YYYY-MM-DD), start_time (HH:MM), end_time (HH:MM, if present), speaker (if present), location, and event_type (use the explicit Type field when present). Time ranges may use either an en dash (–) or a hyphen (-) between start and end.

2) Write a line-delimited JSON file to output/events/events.jsonl containing one JSON object per newly processed file with fields: title, date, start_time, end_time (omit if not present), speaker (omit if not present), location, event_type, source_file (relative path), and source_format ("html" or "md").

3) Also write a CSV to output/events/calendar.csv with a header row and one row per newly processed file with columns in this exact order: date,start_time,title,location,event_type,source_file.

4) Update state by writing output/state/processed.json with a JSON object containing processed_paths as a sorted array that is the union of the paths listed in input/processed_state.json and the newly processed files. Do not duplicate entries in outputs across runs; only emit rows/records for files treated as new in the current run.

Inputs are provided below in input/; please use only these files and the specified paths. Outputs must be written exactly to output/events/events.jsonl, output/events/calendar.csv, and output/state/processed.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
